file_summary: This file implements a command-line requirement tracker with persistent storage. It provides functionality to add, list, search, focus on, complete, set status for, remove, and summarize requirement items. The system stores requirements in a JSON file, supports fuzzy search, status tracking (todo, in-progress, done), thread-based organization, and duplicate detection. It includes a command-line parser with multiple subcommands and handles data persistence through loading/saving operations.

line_windows.summary: Imports, constants, and core store functions: file paths, status labels, store loading/saving, workspace normalization, item retrieval, ID generation, and duplicate detection.
Item filtering and query matching logic: filter by status/query, text normalization, fuzzy matching with overlap ratio, thread ID handling, and thread label formatting.
Timestamp formatting, item sorting, JSON output, and rendering functions: item display, section formatting, and focus view rendering for query-based results.
Completion result rendering and command implementations: add (with duplicate check), list, search, and focus commands for managing requirements.

Contains command functions for list, search, focus, complete, set_status, and remove operations, along with a helper function require_item. Functions handle loading data, filtering items, updating statuses, and saving changes.

Includes the end of cmd_set_status, cmd_remove, cmd_summary, and the beginning of build_parser. The parser setup defines subcommands for add, list, search, focus, complete, set-status, remove, and summary with their respective arguments.

Continues build_parser with argument definitions for complete, set-status, remove, and summary subcommands. Ends with the main function and script entry point that parses arguments and executes the corresponding command function.
